title: salix × dodeana
intro: Salix × dodeana, also known as the dodecandra willow or Indian willow, is a hybrid plant species within the willow family (Salicaceae) that is native to Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Salix × dodeana include relief from pain and fever, as well as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can aid in treating various health conditions, such as arthritis, skin conditions, and infections. Salix × dodeana is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
